agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Order cancellation is a critical lifecycle event in logistics/courier operations. A cancellation reverses a customer's intent to ship, but its feasibility and consequences depend entirely on how far the order has progressed through the fulfillment pipeline. This is not a simple delete — it is a state transition with financial, operational, and physical-world implications.","bounded_context":"Order Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Order","definition":"A customer's request to have a package picked up and delivered to a destination. Has a defined lifecycle with discrete statuses."},{"term":"Order Status","definition":"The current state of an order in its lifecycle (e.g., Pending, Confirmed, Dispatched, In Transit, Out for Delivery, Delivered, Cancelled)."},{"term":"Cancellation","definition":"A customer-initiated request to terminate an order before final delivery. Not a deletion — the order record persists with status Cancelled and an audit trail."},{"term":"Cancellation Window","definition":"The set of order statuses during which cancellation is permitted. Determined by business policy and physical feasibility."},{"term":"Cancellation Fee","definition":"A charge applied when cancellation occurs after resources have already been committed (e.g., courier dispatched)."},{"term":"Refund","definition":"The return of payment to the customer upon successful cancellation, potentially reduced by a cancellation fee."},{"term":"Dispatch","definition":"The act of assigning a courier to pick up an order. A critical inflection point — after dispatch, cancellation has real-world cost."},{"term":"Pickup","definition":"The physical act of a courier collecting the package. After pickup, the package is in the courier's possession."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"An order in status Pending or Confirmed may be cancelled by the customer with no fee.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The fee-free window is a business policy a VP could change (e.g., always charge a fee, or extend the free window).","testable_assertion":"Given an order with status Pending or Confirmed, when the customer cancels, then no cancellation fee is charged and a full refund is issued."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"An order that has been Dispatched but not yet Picked Up may be cancelled, but a cancellation fee applies.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Whether to allow post-dispatch cancellation and the fee amount are business policy decisions.","testable_assertion":"Given an order with status Dispatched, when the customer cancels, then the order is cancelled AND a cancellation fee is deducted from the refund."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"An order that has been Picked Up, is In Transit, or is Out for Delivery cannot be cancelled by the customer.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Once a package is physically in the courier's possession, cancellation is not meaningful — the package exists in the real world and must be handled through a return/redirect process, not cancellation. Allowing cancellation here would corrupt the physical-digital state consistency.","testable_assertion":"Given an order with status In Transit, Picked Up, or Out for Delivery, when the customer attempts to cancel, then the system rejects the cancellation with an explanation."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A Delivered order cannot be cancelled.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Delivery is a terminal state. You cannot cancel something that has already happened. This is a physical constraint.","testable_assertion":"Given an order with status Delivered, when the customer attempts to cancel, then the system rejects the request and suggests the returns process instead."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"An already-Cancelled order cannot be cancelled again.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Cancellation is idempotent from a state perspective — re-cancelling a cancelled order is a logical impossibility and would corrupt audit trails if treated as a new event.","testable_assertion":"Given an order with status Cancelled, when the customer attempts to cancel, then the system returns an error indicating the order is already cancelled."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Every cancellation must record who cancelled, when, the order status at time of cancellation, and the reason (if provided).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Audit trail integrity is non-negotiable for financial reconciliation, dispute resolution, and regulatory compliance. Without it, the system state is unverifiable.","testable_assertion":"Given any successful cancellation, then a cancellation event is persisted containing: actor ID, timestamp, prior order status, and optional reason."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Only the customer who owns the order (or an authorized agent acting on their behalf) may cancel it.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing unauthorized cancellation violates data integrity and trust. One customer cancelling another's order is system corruption.","testable_assertion":"Given a cancellation request where the requesting user does not own the order and is not an authorized agent, then the system rejects the request with a 403/unauthorized error."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"Cancellation must trigger a refund process. The refund amount equals the original payment minus any applicable cancellation fee.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Refund timing, method, and fee structure are business policies that can be adjusted.","testable_assertion":"Given a successful cancellation, then a refund is initiated for (original amount - cancellation fee) within the configured refund SLA."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"An order exists with status Pending","when":"The owning customer requests cancellation","then":"The order status transitions to Cancelled, a full refund is initiated, and a cancellation audit event is recorded"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An order exists with status Confirmed","when":"The owning customer requests cancellation","then":"The order status transitions to Cancelled, a full refund is initiated, and a cancellation audit event is recorded"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An order exists with status Dispatched (courier assigned, not yet picked up)","when":"The owning customer requests cancellation","then":"The order status transitions to Cancelled, the courier assignment is released, a refund minus cancellation fee is initiated, and a cancellation audit event is recorded"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An order exists with status Picked Up, In Transit, or Out for Delivery","when":"The owning customer requests cancellation","then":"The cancellation is rejected with a clear message explaining the order is too far in fulfillment, and the customer is directed to the returns/redirect process"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An order exists with status Delivered","when":"The owning customer requests cancellation","then":"The cancellation is rejected with a clear message, and the customer is directed to the returns process"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An order exists with status Cancelled","when":"The owning customer requests cancellation again","then":"The system returns an idempotent response indicating the order is already cancelled, no duplicate events or refunds are created"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An order exists owned by Customer A","when":"Customer B (not an authorized agent) requests cancellation of that order","then":"The request is rejected with an authorization error and no state change occurs"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A cancellation is successfully processed","when":"The audit log is queried","then":"An event exists with actor ID, timestamp, previous order status, cancellation reason (if provided), and refund amount"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"An order with status Dispatched","when":"The customer cancels and a courier was already en route","then":"The courier is notified of the cancellation so they do not attempt pickup"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A cancellation request is received but the payment refund fails","when":"The refund processor returns an error","then":"The order is still marked Cancelled but the refund is flagged for manual retry; the cancellation is NOT rolled back"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Race condition: Customer cancels while courier simultaneously marks the order as Picked Up","expected_behavior":"The system must use optimistic locking or equivalent concurrency control on order status. Whichever state transition is persisted first wins. If pickup wins, cancellation is rejected. If cancellation wins, the courier is notified the order is cancelled and should not proceed."},{"scenario":"Double-click / duplicate cancellation requests sent in rapid succession","expected_behavior":"The operation must be idempotent. The first request cancels the order; the second returns success but does not create a duplicate audit event or trigger a second refund."},{"scenario":"Customer cancels an order that was placed with a promotional discount or voucher","expected_behavior":"The refund should return the amount actually charged. Whether the voucher/promo is re-credited to the customer is a separate business policy decision that must be defined."},{"scenario":"Partial order cancellation (multi-package or multi-item order)","expected_behavior":"The spec says 'cancel their order' — this implies full order cancellation. If partial cancellation is needed, it requires a separate feature with its own rules. Reject partial cancellation attempts with a clear message."},{"scenario":"Customer cancels during a payment processing hold (payment authorized but not captured)","expected_behavior":"The authorization hold should be voided rather than a refund issued. This is cheaper and faster for both the business and the customer."},{"scenario":"Order is in Confirmed status but transitions to Dispatched between the customer viewing the cancel button and clicking it","expected_behavior":"The system must check status at execution time, not at UI render time. If status changed to Dispatched, the cancellation should still succeed but with the Dispatched-tier rules (cancellation fee applies). The customer must be informed of the fee before confirmation."},{"scenario":"Cancellation requested by a customer support agent on behalf of the customer","expected_behavior":"Permitted if the agent has the appropriate authorization role. The audit event must record both the agent identity and the customer on whose behalf the action was taken."},{"scenario":"Refund to original payment method is impossible (e.g., expired card, closed account)","expected_behavior":"The cancellation still proceeds. The refund is flagged for alternative resolution (store credit, manual bank transfer). Cancellation must not be blocked by refund logistics."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Cancellation directly impacts revenue (refunds reduce realized revenue), courier utilization (dispatched-then-cancelled trips are pure cost), and payment processing fees (refund transactions may incur fees). The cancellation fee for post-dispatch orders partially mitigates courier waste. Without proper controls, abuse of free cancellation could increase operational costs significantly.","operational":"Each cancellation after dispatch wastes courier time and fuel. High cancellation rates in the Dispatched state signal a UX or commitment problem. The audit trail is essential for operations teams to understand cancellation patterns and optimize dispatch timing. Courier notification on cancellation is critical — without it, couriers make wasted trips.","user":"Cancellation is a trust-building feature. Customers who know they can cancel feel more confident placing orders. However, unclear cancellation policies (especially surprise fees) erode trust. The UI must clearly communicate cancellation eligibility and any fees before the customer confirms.","risk":"Primary risks: (1) Race conditions between cancellation and pickup causing inconsistent state, (2) Refund failures leaving customers unpaid, (3) Missing audit trails creating dispute resolution gaps, (4) Authorization gaps allowing malicious cancellation of others' orders. All are mitigable with proper implementation.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Gain ability to cancel orders with clear rules. Positive trust signal. Risk of frustration if fees are unclear or cancellation is rejected unexpectedly."},{"group":"Couriers","impact":"Must receive timely cancellation notifications to avoid wasted trips. Their compensation model for cancelled-after-dispatch orders must be defined."},{"group":"Finance/Accounting","impact":"Must reconcile refunds, cancellation fees, and voided authorizations. Audit trail is their primary dependency."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Will handle edge cases where self-service cancellation fails or is rejected. Need clear escalation paths and override capabilities."},{"group":"Product/Operations","impact":"Cancellation rate becomes a key metric. Need analytics on cancellation reasons, timing, and status-at-cancellation to optimize the funnel."}]},"confidence":{"level":45,"reasoning":"The spec is a single sentence with no detail on cancellation windows, fee structures, refund policies, multi-item orders, or authorization model. All business rules above are inferred from deep logistics domain expertise, but none are confirmed by the product manager. The cancellation fee amounts, refund SLAs, courier compensation policy, and promo/voucher handling are entirely undefined. This analysis is a strong starting point but requires PM validation on every business rule.","high_confidence_areas":["Delivered and already-Cancelled orders cannot be cancelled (physical/logical constraints)","Audit trail is mandatory for any cancellation","Authorization must be enforced — only the order owner can cancel","Cancellation is a state transition, not a deletion"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact cancellation window boundaries (which statuses allow cancellation)","Cancellation fee existence, amount, and when it applies","Refund method and timing policy","Whether partial order cancellation is in scope","Courier compensation for cancelled-after-dispatch orders","Voucher/promo reinstatement policy"],"assumptions":["Order has a linear status progression: Pending → Confirmed → Dispatched → Picked Up → In Transit → Out for Delivery → Delivered","Cancellation fees exist for post-dispatch cancellations (common in logistics but not stated)","Full order cancellation only — partial cancellation is out of scope","Payment was collected or authorized at order creation","The system has a concept of order ownership tied to customer identity"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec does not define which order statuses permit cancellation. The cancellation window is the most critical business decision in this feature and it is entirely unspecified.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Couriers","Finance"],"options":["Allow cancellation only before Dispatch (strictest)","Allow cancellation before Pickup with a fee after Dispatch (balanced)","Allow cancellation at any point before Delivery (most permissive, operationally expensive)"],"recommendation":"Option B (allow before Pickup, fee after Dispatch) — this is industry standard for courier services and balances customer flexibility with operational cost."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"No cancellation fee structure is defined. Does a fee exist? What amount? Flat or percentage? This has direct financial impact.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Finance"],"options":["No cancellation fee ever","Flat fee after dispatch","Percentage of order value after dispatch","Tiered fees based on status proximity to delivery"],"recommendation":"Flat fee after dispatch is simplest to implement and communicate. Exact amount needs PM/Finance input."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Refund timing and method are unspecified. Is the refund immediate or batched? To original payment method only or also store credit?","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Finance"],"options":["Immediate refund to original payment method","Refund within N business days","Store credit option as alternative"],"recommendation":"Immediate void/refund to original payment method where possible, with fallback to store credit if original method is unavailable."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"No mention of courier compensation policy when an order is cancelled after dispatch. Couriers may have already started traveling to the pickup location.","affected_stakeholders":["Couriers","Finance","Operations"],"options":["Courier receives partial compensation for cancelled trips","Courier receives no compensation","Courier receives full trip fee"],"recommendation":"Partial compensation is fairest — exact policy needs Operations input."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec says 'users' — does this include customer support agents acting on behalf of customers, or only the customers themselves via self-service?","affected_stakeholders":["Customer Support","Customers"],"options":["Self-service only","Self-service plus agent-assisted with same rules","Agent-assisted with expanded cancellation window (e.g., can cancel even after pickup)"],"recommendation":"Both self-service and agent-assisted, with agents having the same rules initially. Expanded agent authority can be a follow-up."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Order cancellation is a critical lifecycle event in logistics/courier operations. A cancellation reverses a customer's intent to ship, but its feasibility and consequences depend entirely on how far the order has progressed through the fulfillment pipeline. This is not a simple delete — it is a state transition with financial, operational, and physical-world implications.","bounded_context":"Order Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Order","definit
